interaction.prompt: Your teammate F2 has the puck in the corner. A defender is closing in. What should you do?

feedback.right: Don't crowd the puck. Move to where F3 is — closer to the middle, ready for a pass back to space.
feedback.wrong: Two players on the puck means nobody to pass to. Spread out and give your teammate an option.

tip: When a teammate has the puck, give them an OPTION. Don't run to the puck.
why: Beginners want to chase the puck. The first hockey-sense lesson is the opposite — when your teammate has it, your job is to be available.
